svg.text: QUIC Connection Migration in Neqo (Mozilla Firefox)
How your phone stays connected when switching from WiFi to Cellular
You're watching YouTube on your phone using WiFi at home (IP: 192.168.1.50).
You walk outside and your phone switches to Cellular data (new IP: 10.20.30.40).
With old TCP: Video STOPS, rebuffers, reconnects. With QUIC: Video keeps playing seamlessly!
Here's exactly how Neqo (Firefox's code) makes this magic happen, step by step...
STEP 0: Before Migration - Normal Connection (Everything is fine)
STEP 1: The Trigger - "Oh no, WiFi is gone!"
1. You walk out of WiFi range
force: false // validate first, don't just trust it
STEP 2: Create a New Path - "Let me try this new road"
Same destination, different road to get there!
Neqo keeps BOTH roads until the new one is tested.
What Neqo does in this step:
STEP 3: PATH_CHALLENGE - "Hey server, can you hear me on this new road?"
Why does Neqo need to check? (Security!)
A bad guy could pretend to have a different IP address and trick the server into sending data there.
So Neqo sends a SECRET RANDOM NUMBER (8 bytes) to the server and says: "Send this number back to prove you got it!"
// a NEW address but SAME CID!
"CID ABC123 came from a new IP"
"Hey server! Send back this secret number to prove you hear me!"
What the actual packet looks like on the wire:
STEP 4: PATH_RESPONSE - "Yes! I can hear you! Here's your number back!"
"Here's your secret number back! I got your message!"
Detailed code flow when PATH_RESPONSE arrives:
2. frame.rs: decode PATH_RESPONSE frame // extract the 8 bytes
3. path.rs: Path::path_response(response_bytes) // compare with sent challenge
4. path.rs: Path::set_valid(now) // MATCH! Mark path as valid!
5. path.rs: Paths::path_response() --> returns true // migration_target matched!
6. path.rs: Paths::select_primary(new_path) // SWITCH to new path!
STEP 5: Switch! - "New road works! Let's use it!"
old_path.set_primary(false, now); // old WiFi path is no longer primary
self.paths.swap(0, idx); // put new cellular path in slot 0 (protected)
new_path.set_primary(true, now); // cellular is now the boss!
STEP 6: Done! Video keeps playing! You didn't notice anything!
Video data flowing on NEW road!
Same Connection ID, different IP - YouTube doesn't care!
THE COMPLETE CODE FLOW (All Neqo files involved)
"WiFi is gone, switch to cellular!" --> calls connection.migrate()
Short Header packet | Connection ID: ABC123 | Encrypted payload | Padded to 1200+ bytes
AES-128-GCM / ChaCha20-Poly1305 | Everything after header is encrypted | Firewall can't read it!
LAYER 8: UDP Socket (neqo-udp/src/lib.rs) --> Sent to the internet!
WHAT THE FIREWALL SEES (This is why the attack works!)
(source IP changed! This is the migration)
THEY LOOK IDENTICAL! The firewall CANNOT tell them apart!
TIMELINE: How fast does this happen?
Total time: ~70ms (0.07 seconds!) - You blinked and missed it!
Same thing with TCP: ~2000ms+ (2+ seconds of buffering!)
